title: A confusion between the releases of the serie
display_title: A confusion between the releases of the serie
wikitext:
[]: A confusion between the releases of the serie             Quote Post by joseg5 » Thu Dec 29, 2016 12:21 am Hello! I'm new in the forum, but i was a leecher since a pair of years i'm latin and i saw the serie in the TV when i was a child (have a good dubbing), later i see the movies and Death and Rebirth, but now i'm seeing the series again and i want to see the version without censure and complete, trying to get that i download the Renewal version (Japanese voice and eng/esp subs). The problem starts when i notice that some scenes doesn't appear in the chapters by example this image http://wiki.evageeks.org/File:22_C391_asuka-alone.jpg from the chapter 9. My question is if the version that i'm watching is the real renewal or if is better that i try to download another version for my purpose.

[]: Re: A confusion between the releases of the serie             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 29, 2016 3:07 am That scene appears as a flashback in the extended ("Director's Cut") version of episode 22, rather than when it actually happens. Only episodes 21 to 24 were augmented with extra material, to lead into End of Evangelion.

Article: A confusion between the releases of the serie
[]: A confusion between the releases of the serie             Quote Post by joseg5 » Thu Dec 29, 2016 12:21 am Hello! I'm new in the forum, but i was a leecher since a pair of years i'm latin and i saw the serie in the TV when i was a child (have a good dubbing), later i see the movies and Death and Rebirth, but now i'm seeing the series again and i want to see the version without censure and complete, trying to get that i download the Renewal version (Japanese voice and eng/esp subs). The problem starts when i notice that some scenes doesn't appear in the chapters by example this image http://wiki.evageeks.org/File:22_C391_asuka-alone.jpg from the chapter 9. My question is if the version that i'm watching is the real renewal or if is better that i try to download another version for my purpose.

[]: Re: A confusion between the releases of the serie             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 29, 2016 3:07 am That scene appears as a flashback in the extended ("Director's Cut") version of episode 22, rather than when it actually happens. Only episodes 21 to 24 were augmented with extra material, to lead into End of Evangelion.
